Um How quickly we're adding new units.
Um So we bring a bunch of data to the table.
But the key difference from radix is that Radix relies on a network, right?
So you have the network effect where you're limited to how many properties that's one side, the other side being um the whole conversation around public versus private data.
Um You may have seen that lawsuit that happened earlier this summer.
Um I think with real Page, it was they're the first ones to get it.
Uh where we're talking about the uh transmission of private data uh across market surveys.
Uh Here, apartment IQ only uses publicly available information.
So our method of pulling this data is uniform and it's consistent and standardized every time because we're pulling this directly from competitor websites and landlord websites.
So as long as a landlord at capital club.com, right, uh has availability has unit information.
We pull that.
So no one's ever getting in trouble about, hey, is this private data like, are we allowed to know this?
And is there any issues there?
So that's probably the key difference and then the the other difference being availability of information, right?
Um Radi, my understanding and again, I'm not 100% familiar with RADI.
Um But my understanding is that the update happens approximately once a week, right, where everyone's asked to input their information.
Uh apart apartment IQ is every day, we pull this information every evening so that whenever you need it, it is always the most up to date and available information possible.
So those are probably the three key differentiators that I would say.
Ok,
so we're coming straight from what a landlord feels comfortable posting and therefore it's what renters are acting on, right?
These are the same insights that your most data savvy and most data forward renter would be collecting themselves and making decisions on.
So it puts you and your leasing team in their, in their headspace kind of and gets you closer to how they make their decisions.
